meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Student Developers: Solve Marketing Gaps
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing to bypass marketing hurdles. A beginner-friendly case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first portfolio-ready app.
content.answer_block: If you lack marketing skills, leverage smart SaaS pricing as your primary growth engine. By implementing a value-based pricing model that targets specific niches, you turn your product into its own marketer. Use Antigravity to structure the architecture and Claude Code to deploy the payment logic seamlessly and rapidly.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding the perfect feature, but the silence from the market is deafening. You aren't a marketer, and the thought of 'hustling' on social media feels like a barrier to entry that might kill your project before it starts. The truth? You don't need a massive marketing budget if your pricing strategy acts as your product's sales funnel.

Great pricing isn't just about numbers; it's about signaling value to the right user. By building a frictionless, tiered pricing model into your MVP, you transform passive visitors into paying customers. This guide shows you how to use modern AI tools to automate the technical side of monetization, letting your code do the selling for you.

steps.title: Define Your Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, define three tiers: Entry (problem solver), Pro (workflow optimizer), and Enterprise (scale). This anchors your pricing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS pricing strategist. Create a JSON schema for a three-tier pricing model (Free, Pro, Enterprise) for a productivity SaaS. Include feature sets, monthly pricing, and value propositions for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a pricing-tiers.json file based on the Antigravity schema provided.'

steps.title: Architect the Payment Gateway
steps.description: Integrate Stripe to handle transactions without building complex backend infrastructure from scratch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular Stripe checkout integration using TypeScript. Focus on a secure session creation endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add stripe-sdk --prompt 'Implement the checkout session logic in the src/api/payments.ts file'

steps.title: Implement Webhook Logic
steps.description: Ensure your app reacts to subscription status changes automatically to manage access levels.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Node.js webhook handler for Stripe events (customer.subscription.created, customer.subscription.deleted).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a webhook endpoint in src/api/webhooks.ts and ensure it validates signatures.'

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI
steps.description: Create a high-converting pricing table that highlights the 'Pro' tier as the clear choice.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React component for a pricing table using Tailwind CSS. Include a 'Most Popular' badge for the middle tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ui --prompt 'Add the PricingTable component to the landing page and style it with Tailwind.'

steps.title: Automate Deployment
steps.description: Deploy your app to production so you can start testing your pricing strategy with real users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script for a Vercel-ready Next.js app, including environment variable configurations for Stripe keys.

pro_tips: Always price your entry tier low enough to remove the 'friction of curiosity' for students.
Use 'Price Anchoring' by making your middle tier the most attractive option through clear feature differentiation.
Never build custom billing logic; always use Stripe or LemonSqueezy to handle tax compliance automatically.
Implement a 'Trial-to-Paid' automated email sequence immediately upon sign-up to capture users who didn't convert.
Update your pricing every 3 months based on user feedback; it is a living variable, not a fixed constant.
content.next_steps: Now that your pricing is live, focus on building one 'killer feature' that justifies your Pro tier price. Collect feedback from the first five users and iterate on the value proposition.
content.conversion_block: You have the technical foundation and the pricing strategy to stop worrying about marketing and start focusing on product-market fit. Stop building in isolation and join a community that understands the intersection of code and commerce. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
